title: FAA Scraps Civil and Criminal Penalties for Flying Drones Near ICE Vehicles

title: Texas Medical Board Sanctions Three Doctors for Delayed Care That Led to the Deaths of Two Pregnant Women
summary: The post Texas Medical Board Sanctions Three Doctors for Delayed Care That Led to the Deaths of Two Pregnant Women appeared first on ProPublica .
reason: The sanctions reveal how the post-Dobbs legal architecture functions as an alibi structure — doctors delayed life-saving care not from incompetence but because the law made the cost of acting higher than the cost of waiting, making the state's 'medical exception' defense visibly hollow.
angle: Use the alibi structure tool: the 'medical exception' in abortion bans requires you to believe doctors can make autonomous clinical decisions, but these cases show the exception is designed to fail, and the sanctions make that alibi briefly visible as an alibi.
